title: Terak
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Youmay|the [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] named Terak|the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] named [[Terrak Morrhage]]}}
{{Character
|type=Criminal
|image=[[File:Terak-Chron.png]]
|name=Terak
|homeworld=[[Sanyassa IV]]<ref name="dbter">{{DB|character|terak|Terak}}</ref>
|birth=Prior to [[96 BBY]]<ref name="CSWE">[[Charal]]'s entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' described her stranding on Endor as lasting nearly a century, meaning that Terak's birth and exile must had occourred by the year [[96 BBY]].</ref>
|death=[[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]], [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]<ref name="BFE">''[[Ewoks: The Battle for Endor]]''</ref>
|species=[[Sanyassan/Legends|Sanyassan]]<ref name="dbter" />
|gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]<ref name="dbter" />
|pronouns=new
|height=2.3 [[Meter/Legends|meters]]<ref name="dbter" />
|mass=
|hair=[[Color/Legends|White]]<ref name="BFE" />
|feathers=
|eyes=Gray<ref name="BFE" />
|skin=Green-gray<ref name="BFE" />
|cyber=
|families=new
|parents=new
|partners=new
|siblings=new
|children=*Several sons<ref name="GORW"/>
**[[Zakul]]<ref name="GORW"/>
|affiliation=[[Sanyassan Marauders]]<ref name="BFE" />
|masters=
|apprentices=
}}
{{Quote|He will get what he desires.|Charal|<ref name="BFE" />}}
'''Terak''' was a [[Sanyassan/Legends|Sanyassan]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] from the storm-shrouded [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Sanyassa IV]]. Huge and fierce-looking even by Sanyassan standards, he was the chief of the [[Sanyassan Marauders|Marauders]], a band of criminals who engaged in spaceways [[Pirate/Legends|piracy]]. A [[Standard century/Legends|century]] before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Terak and his Marauders joined with [[Charal]], a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Forceful]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] who possessed a [[Talisman of the Raven|magic ring]]. Traveling through the [[Moddell sector/Legends|Moddell sector]] in a stolen [[Starship/Legends|starship]], they crashed on the lush [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. Resigned to their fate, the Marauders constructed a [[Castle/Legends|castle]] in the plains of Endor, where they were ruled by the self-appointed King Terak, and Charal his accomplice.

In the months leading to his destruction, Terak [[Death/Legends|murdered]] most of the [[Towani family|Towani]], a family of [[Human/Legends|Humans]] who were themselves castaways on Endor. The king wished to obtain the [[crystal oscillator]] from their spacecraft, as he believed it to be "the Power" he was looking for. Having captured the crystal, Terak had no idea how to release its energy. He tried to compel the young [[Cindel Towani]], last survivor of the Towani family, into revealing the secrets of the crystal. But the little girl was unable to answer the king's questions, and he [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] her.

The [[Standard day/Legends|day]] after her capture, Cindel was freed from the Marauder's dungeons by her peaceful Endorian friends. Terak ordered the witch Charal to transform into a [[Raven/Legends|raven]] to lead him to the Humans, so he could retrieve the crystal. The warlord kept the Nightsister's magic ring to guarantee she would not betray him. This, however, led Terak to his ultimate demise; during a pitched fight, the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] hero [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Warrick]] struck the ring with a stone, causing it to activate, and the evil Marauder was burnt to a crisp.

==Biography==
===Early life===
{{Quote|There was a time when each of those who you see before you were kings of the stars. We rode great warships in the vastness of space, terrorizing the galaxies with the mere mention of our name.|Terak to a spacer|<ref name="gal">{{GalaxiesAED}}</ref>}}
[[File:Sanyassans_iswu.jpg|thumb|left|Back on Sanyassa IV, Terak assumed leadership of the Marauders.|270x270px]]
[[Reproduction/Legends|Born]] on the gloomy planet Sanyassa IV<ref name="dbter" /> more than a century before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]],<ref name="CSWE" /> Terak was a male Sanyassan.<ref name="dbter" /> On his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], hierarchy was essentially determined by force and stature.<ref name="dbYavid">{{DB|character|generalyavid|Yavid, General}}</ref> As Terak grew to become unusually tall, even by reference to his species's standards, he ended up reaching the top of the social pyramid and became a warlord.<ref name="WWSWG">{{InsiderCite|65|Who's Who in Star Wars Galaxies|Who's Who in ''Star Wars'' Galaxies}}</ref> Terak assumed the leadership of the [[Sanyassan Marauders|Marauders]], a band of Sanyassan criminals. The warlord imagined that he and his goons could become great space pirates and the scourge of their native Moddell sector. However, their reign of terror proved much shorter than the warlord had expected. The Marauders had been successful in stealing a spaceship, but none of them were able to pilot it properly.<ref name="ISWU">''[[The Illustrated Star Wars Universe]]''</ref> Hunted by [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|law enforcement agencies]], they kept moving through the sector, until they met a strange woman known as Charal—a [[Nightsisters/Legends|Nightsister]] [[Witch/Legends|witch]] from [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]]. She possessed a magic ring that allowed her to [[Metamorphosis|transform]] into a raven at will. Impressed by her strange [[Force power/Legends|powers]], Terak agreed to take Charal aboard and established her as his personal mystic.<ref name="dbchar">{{DB|character|charal|Charal}}</ref>
[[File:Terakskeep.jpg|thumb|270x270px|[[Terak's Keep]], a castle built on [[Endor/Legends|Endor]].]]
Around [[96 BBY]],<ref name="CSWE" /> when the Marauders' ship reached the [[Endor system/Legends|Endor system]], the improvised navigator couldn't handle the unusal [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] patterns in the area, as the vessel was way too advanced for him to pilot. The Sanyassans pirates crash landed on the [[Tree/Legends|forest]] [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of Endor,<ref name="ISWU" /> a celestial body orbiting a planet known as [[Endor (planet)/Legends|Tana]].<ref name="AGTSWU2">''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''</ref> Many Marauders survived the crash, including the navigator, but Terak blamed him for his failure and had him executed in a fit of rage.<ref name="ISWU" /> As their ship was damaged beyond repair,<ref name=":2">{{GamerCite|9|Endor and the Moddell Sector}}</ref> the Marauders resigned to their fate, and a new life began for them in the plains of Endor. Terak established himself as [[King/Legends|King]] of the Marauders, with Charal as his cohort.<ref name="dbsan" /> They relocated in the open savanna<ref name=":2" /> where the built a squarish [[Terak's Keep|stronghold]], which would serve both as barracks and fortified residence of Terak.<ref name="dbsan">{{DB|species|marauder|Sanyassan Marauders}}</ref> The castle was built with stone quarried from mines in the [[Dragon's Spine (mountain chain)|Dragon's Spine]]’s foothills.<ref name="GORW" /> For his throne, Terak tore the captain’s chair from the bridge of the dilapidated ship and placed it in his royal audience chamber.<ref name="ISWU" />

Terak held his mercenary pirates together and, under the lead of their king, the Sanyassans worked to bring terror to the other [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] of Endor, the [[Bear/Legends|bear]]-like Ewoks and the singing [[Yuzzum/Legends|Yuzzum]].<ref name="ISWU" /> It became one of the planet's biggest scourges, discussed only in whispers in [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] villages and [[Yuzzum/Legends|Yuzzum]] burrows alike,<ref name="GORW" /> and a constant source of strife and conflict.<ref name=":2" /> At some point he had also fathered several sons incluning [[Zakul]], who would grow up to match his father's brutality.<ref name="GORW">''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]''</ref>

===Life on the Forest Moon===
{{Quote|Destroy any who defend the ship. Do what you will with them, only bring us the power. Make a thorough search of the area and all beings from the ship, bring tools, parts, documents, anything you find. I can feel our inevitable return to the glory of the stars coming closer, closer. I have only to reach out and... |Terak to a spacer|<ref name="gal" />}}
[[File:Nobonesaboutit.jpg|left|thumb|270x270px|The remains of [[Salak Weet]], a victim of Terak's ignorance.]]
With his dreams of glory shattered, Terak came to hate the Forest Moon, which he called a "green carbuncle on the backside of the galaxy."<ref name="gal" /> Around the year [[26 BBY/Legends|26 BBY]],<ref>{{HoloNetNewsWeb|49|regional/13328_2.html|Missing Starship Search Abandoned}}</ref> the Marauders captured the stranded<ref name="dbter" /> [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]]<ref name="BFE" /> scout [[Salak Weet]], who told them about his damaged starship hidden in the forests. Weet explained how the starcruiser traveled through space thanks to the "power" of a [[crystal oscillator]].<ref name="dbter" /> Terak knew nothing about spaceships, and he thought a crystal oscillator was a magic [[Gemstone/Legends|gemstone]] that could give him great powers.<ref name="RWC" /> He [[Death/Legends|killed]] Weet in an attempt to gain that power.<ref name="BFE" /> When the murder accomplished nothing, the Marauders embarked on a quest to locate Weet's ship and the great power they presumed was contained therein.<ref name="dbsan" /> Unbeknownst to the Marauders, Veet had not come alone to Endor. His human partner [[Noa Briqualon]] awaited Veet's return for a time. When Briqualon understood that Salak Veet was probably dead, he settled as a hermit in the forest and maintained a low profile for many years on.<ref name="BFE" />

Terak expected the solution to their exile to be a simple one. However, in time, he himself did not remember why he was searching for a power pack, but he was obsessed with getting the power, even though he would not know what to do with it if he held it in his hands.<ref name="ISWU" /> Terak possessed an ancient scroll that he believed would one day gave him the power to conquer the star as he had “conquered" [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. All he needed to do was unlock the scroll's secrets. He concluded that what he needed was a source of power, but he did not realize that his ancient scroll was a blueprint for a starship, and the Power was literally the energy matrix that would allow his starship to leave Endor.<ref name="WWSWG" />

====Enlisting the spacer====
{{Quote|Scholar Szingo tells me you are worthy of trust, but you look like a weak willed, oafish lout, used to failing in your duties. We will quickly find the value of appearance.|Terak to a spacer|<ref name="gal" />}}
[[File:King_Terak_SWGal.jpg|thumb|150px|King Terak in the early days of the Galactic Civil War]]
In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="SWG_timeline">{{SWG_timeline}}</ref> Terak sent out a patrol that was ambushed by Ewoks around the border of their territories. The only survivor, a pink-skinned Sanyassan named [[Indar (Marauder)|Indar]], reported their defeat was due to a single Ewok holyman wielding a powerful [[magic wand]]. The warlord hoped this scepter contained the elusive "Power" that would help him leave Endor. King Terak seized the opportunity. He bartered with a passing-by [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]], sending the offworlder on a mission to kill the Ewok priest and return to the [[Marauder's Stronghold]] with his magic metal staff. When the spacer returned with the staff, it proved to be a sham. Terak understood that Indar had lied to him in hopes of covering up his cowardice during the ambush with Ewok warriors. The warlord was incensed about this act of treachery, and he ordered the spacer to retrieve and kill Indar.<ref name="gal" />

A bit later, Terak's scouts reported a ship had crashed not far from the Stronghold. The king ordered the spacer to raid the shipwreck and take anything he could find, especially potential sources of power. When the spacer returned with a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] generator, Terak was overjoyed. The warlord immediately ordered the Sanyassan [[Academician|scholar]] [[Szingo]] to study it carefully. As a last assignment Terak also tasked the spacer with stopping two Ewok warriors who intended to raid the Stronghold, abundantly insisting that the matter should be resolved in the most violent and bloody manner.<ref name="gal" />

====The Emperor's scouts====
{{Quote|Terak, the king of the marauders, holds his mercenary pirates together while he search for a new power source to be installed on their long-disassembled attack ship.|Pfilbee Jhorn, on Terak|<ref name="ISWU" />}}
Several years before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]<ref name="ISWU" /> in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref>[[The New Essential Chronology|''The New Essential Chronology'']]</ref> the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] sent a scouting expedition to Endor, where [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] wished to build the [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generator]] for his [[Death Star II/Legends|new]] [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]]. Terak and his Marauders captured the scouting party, which included the [[Sergeant/Legends|sergeant]] [[Pfilbee Jhorn]]. While the Imperial team contemplated an alliance with the Marauders, Terak and his goons did not see it that way. The King ordered the Imperials brought to his dungeons, where a few of them were [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] to death. While his prisoners were still in jail, the Sanyassan warlord discovered that his mystic Charal had plotted behind his back. The Dathomiri had freed the Imperials for a promise to take her offworld with them. Outraged by that act of [[Treason/Legends|treason]], Terak put Charal behind bars for a time.<ref name="ISWU" />

===The Battle for Endor===
{{Quote|How dare you suggest that I am to blame?|Terak|<ref name="BFE" />}}
[[File:Terak battleforendor.jpg|thumb|270x270px|Terak leads his attack at the [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]].]]
Terak's obsession with obtaining what he called the "Power" increased over the years. About a century after Terak's arrival on the moon, by the year [[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]],<ref>''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]''</ref> a family of [[Human/Legends|Humans]] called the [[Towani family|Towani]] were stranded on the Forest Moon.<ref>[[Caravan of Courage: An Ewok Adventure|''Caravan of Courage: An Ewok Adventure'']]</ref> Several months later,<ref name=":0">[[Ewoks: The Battle for Endor (book-and-record)|''Ewoks: The Battle for Endor'' book-and-record]]</ref> Terak and the sorceress Charal led a [[Raid on Bright Tree Village|raid]]<ref name="BFE" /> at the morning<ref name=":0" /> on [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]], where the Towani were sheltered.<ref name="BFE" /> Terak and his Marauders had mistook the stranded star cruiser of the Towani for Salak's ship.<ref name="dbter" />

Terak confronted the father, [[Jeremitt Towani]], who was trying to repair his ship, and demanded the "Power." The king seized the power cell from the starcruiser and killed the unarmed Human. Jeremitt's daughter, the young [[Cindel Towani]], was able to escape while Ewoks were gathered up to be taken back to Terak's castle. The girl's escape, however, was short-lived—she was captured by Charal and placed in a prison carriage with her Ewok friend Wicket Warrick. When he looked at his young Human [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]], Terak thought Cindel was "a pretty prize." With the other Ewoks' help, Towani and Warrick escaped from the carriage, but Terak only sent a few Marauders after them.<ref name="BFE" />[[File:Terak_throne.png|thumb|left|303x303px|Terak on his throne with the crystal oscillator]]At the Marauders' keep, King Terak wanted Charal to get the oscillator crystal to work by chanting over it, to no avail. According to the witch, her spies had located Cindel Towani in the woods, near the [[Noa Briqualon's hut|hut]] of the old scout Noa Briqualon. She suggested the young girl might know the secret of the magic crystal and could be forced to share it with Terak. The Sanyassan king agreed, and he ordered the witch to bring the child back. When Towani was taken before him,<ref name="BFE" /> an evily grinning<ref name="RWC" /> Terak ordered her to release the "Power," but she answered the crystal was not magic and was only a machine part. But Terak did not believe her, and he had both the girl and Charal taken to the dungeon with the Ewoks captives. The king threatened to execute all the prisoners if Towani had not shown the secret of the crystal before dawn.<ref name="BFE" />

That night, the Marauders held a banquet in castle hall. Terak remained seated on his [[Throne/Legends|throne]] with a pint of alcohol, hoping the little girl would eventually change her mind. Suddenly, an alarm bell started ringing from the dungeons, meaning the prisoners had escaped. Terak headed for the cellblocks, even as the prisoners were blasting a hole in the wall to escape through. The king learned that Cindel and the Ewoks had been freed by their friends, Noa Briqualon, Wicket Warrick and a creature known as Teek. The enraged warlord then freed Charal to help him trace the fugitives back. The sorceress used her ring and turned into a raven, but Terak grabbed the bird by the leg and seized the ring so Charal would not run away and betray him.<ref name="BFE" />

[[File:Terak_burning.png|thumb|270x270px|King Terak was burned to his death]]
The Marauders army eventually found the fugitives, but the Ewoks put up a [[Battle of Endor (Ewok–Sanyassan conflict)|valiant defense]] while Noa Briqualon was trying to get his starship running using the energy cell from the Towani spacecraft. During the skirmish, Cindel Towani was captured by Terak, even as the other Marauders retreated. A climactic duel ensued between the towering Sanyassan and Briqualon. The old hermit was able to strike many good blows, but Terak ended up chopping Briqualon's staff in two with his sword. When it seemed that the warlord had won the fight, Wicket Warrick used his [[Slingshot/Legends|slingshot]] and damaged Charal's ring, which Terak wore in a pendant round his neck. The talisman shattered, releasing a great amount of energy,<ref name="BFE" /> and the Marauder king was burned from the inside-out.<ref name="dbter" />

===Legacy===
Because Terak died before returning the ring,<ref name="dbchar" /> Charal was trapped in her animal form forever<ref name=":1" /> and was unable to reclaim the remains of her ring.<ref>[[The Official Star Wars Fact File 123|''The Official Star Wars Fact File'' 123]] <small>(TOW 1-4: Cindel Towani)</small></ref>

Terak's death created a power vacuum in the Marauders' society. While his [[General/Legends|General]] [[Yavid]] did attempt to succeed him as king, but he failed to bring the rabble-rousers into line.<ref name="dbYavid" /> Ultimately, Terak's son [[Zakul]] managed to impose his authority over the Marauders and acquiring the throne.<ref name="GORW" /> Nevertheless, when the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] later sent [[Agent/Legends|agents]] to scout the Endorian forest, they found no trace of the Sanyassan Marauders.<ref name="FF10">{{FactFile|10|END 1-4|The Forest Moon of Endor}}</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Silence, fool. You will perform this task for the simple reason I told you to do it. And if you fail me in this it may be my own men who pursue you to your just reward.|Terak, to a spacer|<ref name="gal" />}}
[[File:Cindel10.jpg|left|thumb|270x270px|Terak ready to harm [[Cindel Towani]].]]
At 2.3 [[Meter/Legends|meters]],<ref name="dbter" /> Terak was a giant,<ref>''[[George Lucas: The Creative Impulse]]''</ref> taller and more powerful than many of his peers.<ref name="WWSWG" /> Terak was also long-lived and was able to live for a hundred years on Endor,<ref name="CSWE" /> though eventually grew bored of the forest moon.<ref name="gal" /> Even before his arrival at Endor, he was a vicious and bloodthirsty warlord<ref name="ISWU" /> and became obsessed with obtaining what he called the "Power," which he was convinced would allow him to leave Endor and conquer the galaxy. Fortunately for the rest of the galaxy, Terak's strength was not equaled by his intelligence. His way of thinking was more in the superstitious form than the scientific form. Otherwise he could have simply taken the Towanis' ship. Instead, Terak believed beyond a shadow of a doubt that the oscillator by itself was the key to letting him and the other Marauders leave Endor. Terak also had a dark sense of humor, which he shared with his subjects.<ref name="BFE" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Terak_concept_art.png|thumb|369x369px|Early concept art of Terak sitting on his throne]]
The Marauder King Terak was created by [[George Lucas]] for [[Jim Wheat|Jim]] and [[Ken Wheat]]'s [[1985]] made-for-television film ''[[Ewoks: The Battle for Endor]]''.<ref name="SWArchive-Ewok Adventures: A Television Adventure">{{SWArchive|url=eu/tv/classic/f20000824/index.html|text=Ewok Adventures: A Television Adventure}}</ref> In the movie, 2.13-meter-tall<ref name="IMDB">{{IMDb|url=name/nm0835393|text=Carel Struycken}}</ref> [[Wikipedia:Netherlands|Dutch]] actor [[Carel Struycken]] portrayed Terak.<ref name="BFE" /> In early designs by the American director and artist [[Joe Johnston]], Terak was one-eyed and bald, with a thinner frame, yellowish skin and three-fingered hands.<ref name="ISWU" />

The [[2003]] article "[[Who's Who in Star Wars Galaxies]]," published in [[Star Wars Insider 65|''Star Wars Insider'' 65]], claimed that Terak's quest for the "Power" caused him to forge his alliance with Charal.<ref name="WWSWG" /> However, both Charal's [[Databank (original)|Databank]] entry on [[StarWars.com]]<ref name="dbchar" /> and ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', published in [[2008]],<ref name="CSWE" /> stated that the witch joined the Marauders shortly before their crash on Endor, which happened decades before Terak first heard of the "Power" from Salak Weet. This article follows the facts featured in the ''Complete Encyclopedia'', as it is the newer source.

The sources also give conflicting accounts of Terak's demise. While the King's defunct [[Databank (original)|Databank]] entry on [[StarWars.com]] stated that he was burned by the power of the ring,<ref name="dbter" /> the reference book [[The Essential Guide to Characters|''The Essential Guide to Characters'']], the young readers' book ''[[The Ring, the Witch, and the Crystal: An Ewok Adventure]]'' and the book-and-record [[Ewoks: The Battle for Endor (book-and-record)|''Ewoks: The Battle for Endor'']] stated that he was turned to [[Stone/Legends|stone]] instead.<ref name=":1">''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref><ref name="RWC">''[[The Ring, the Witch, and the Crystal: An Ewok Adventure]]''</ref><ref name=":0" /> This article follows the Databank's version of the facts, as it was once the most official ''[[Star Wars]]'' database.
